CSEC 701 · 3 Credit Hours · Premium Track
Information Security Fundamentals
Exam: CompTIA Security+ SY0-701 Start: Jun 8, 2026 Target: Oct 5, 2026 Passing: 750 / 900
📋
Study Requirements: Attend 2–3 lectures or one chapter section per session. Write one sentence of notes per lecture in your own words. Add new vocabulary to your Review Lab. Sessions run 45–60 minutes, five days per week.
🔁
Attendance Policy: Missed sessions do not require restarting the course. Locate the current week in the Academic Schedule, resume from the next incomplete session. All deadlines remain fixed — the path forward is always the next lecture.

📅
18-week plan · 5 days/week · 45–60 min/session. Start date: June 8, 2026. Exam target: October 5, 2026. Each week has fixed dates — current week auto-opens. Overdue days are flagged in red; just pick up from the next one.
The Approach
💡
With $200–$300/month, you can run the premium stack: CompTIA's own CertMaster platform for adaptive learning and labs, all of Messer's materials, official study guide, and both Dion + Messer practice exam sets. This gives you 5 separate ways to test knowledge before the real exam — more than enough to hit 85%+ consistently.
Phase Timeline
1
Phase 1 — Warm-Up: Google Cybersecurity Certificate
~4–6 weeks · Free via Blacks in Technology · Start now
Courses 1, 2, 3, 5, and 6 only. Builds the mental model before Messer. Skip course 4 (Linux/SQL), 7 (Python), 8 (job prep), 9 (AI) — not relevant to Security+.
2
Phase 2 — Core: Professor Messer SY0-701 + CertMaster
~9–12 weeks · CertMaster $149/mo or $299/yr · Start alongside Phase 1 or right after
121 free videos are the backbone. CertMaster Learn ($149/mo) runs in parallel — its adaptive questions and performance tracker show exactly which topics are weak. Activate CertMaster for 1 month during heavy study, then pause or cancel.
3
Phase 3 — Reinforce: Sybex + GCGA + Anki
Runs concurrent with Phase 2 · Both free (you have ebook access) · Chapter-a-week pace
You have both — use them together. Read the Sybex chapter for deep conceptual grounding, then check the GCGA chapter for exam-style practice questions on the same domain. Best of both worlds at no extra cost.
4
Phase 4 — Exam Prep: Practice Exam Gauntlet
~3–4 weeks · Jason Dion $15 + Messer exams $35
Three exam sets: Dion (6 exams, harder than real thing), Messer (closest to real exam style), and Sybex (included in book). Don't schedule the real exam until hitting 85%+ across at least 3 full practice sets on separate days.
✓
Exam — CompTIA Security+ SY0-701
$425 via Pearson VUE (or ~$360–380 via authorized partner) · Schedule when 85%+ is consistent · Deadline: May 2027
750/900 to pass. Book the exam when you hit the score threshold — not before. The May 2027 retirement gives you buffer. You have time to be thorough.
Domain Weights (Actual CompTIA)
D1 · General Security Concepts12%
D2 · Threats, Vulnerabilities & Mitigations22%
D3 · Security Architecture18%
D4 · Security Operations28%
D5 · Security Program Management & Oversight20%
Full Resource Stack
💰
Budget summary: ~$180 in study materials + $425 exam fee = ~$605 total (or ~$540–560 via authorized partner voucher). With $200–$300/month, you can front-load the materials in month 1 and bank the rest toward the exam. Tip: buy your voucher through a CompTIA authorized partner for ~$360–380 instead of the full $425 retail price.
Resource Cost Phase Notes
Google Cybersecurity Certificate
Blacks in Technology · Open on Coursera →		 Free P1 Free through Blacks in Technology. Courses 1, 2, 3, 5, 6 only.
Professor Messer SY0-701 Video Course
professormesser.com · YouTube Playlist →		 Free P2 121 videos, ~15 hours. Core study resource. Watch actively — take notes.
Anki SY0-701 — Personal Security+ Vocab
307 terms · Download .apkg →		 Free P2 307 terms. Run 10 min/day throughout Phase 2.
Anki SY0-701 Acronym Deck
321 acronyms · Download .apkg →		 Free P2 321 acronyms. Add to daily Anki rotation once started.
CompTIA Security+ Study Guide, 9th Ed. (Sybex)
Mike Chapple & David Seidl · Open in Drive →		 Free P2 Deep conceptual reference — read chapter-per-domain alongside Messer. Includes practice exam. Use for understanding; pair with GCGA for exam-style questions on the same material.
Get Certified Get Ahead (GCGA) SY0-701
Gibson & Shelley · Open in Drive →		 Free P2 Mirrors CompTIA's question style and phrasing closely. Use end-of-chapter practice Qs after each Messer domain. Strong on PBQs. Use alongside Sybex — you have both free.
Professor Messer SY0-701 Course Notes
96 pages · Open in Drive →		 Free P2 PDF mirroring every video. Good for reviewing without rewatching. Optional but efficient.
CompTIA CertMaster Learn SY0-701
comptia.org		 $149/mo or $299/yr P2 CompTIA's official adaptive platform. Performance analytics show exact weak domains. Activate for 1 month during heaviest study — cancel after. Labs included.
Jason Dion Practice Exams — Set 1
6 exams · 540 Qs · Udemy →		 $13.99 P4 6 full 90-min timed exams, 90 Qs each including PBQs. Harder than the real exam — intentional. Per-question feedback with domain mapping.
Jason Dion Practice Exams — Set 2
6 exams · 540 Qs · Udemy →		 $16.99 P4 Completely different question set from Set 1. Same format — 6 exams × 90 Qs, timed, with PBQs and per-question domain feedback.
Professor Messer Practice Exams (A, B, C)
v1.6 · 3 × 90 Qs · Open in Drive →		 Free P4 Closest to real exam style and phrasing. Different question set from Dion — together they cover a wide range of material angles.
CompTIA Security+ SY0-701 Exam
pearsonvue.com/comptia		 $425 retail
~$360–380 via partner		 EXAM Schedule only after hitting 85%+ across 3+ full practice sets on different days. 750/900 to pass.

Chapter-to-Domain Mapping
🗺️
Read the Sybex and GCGA chapters that match the Messer domain you're on. Don't read linearly — follow the domain order below.
Domain Sybex Ch. GCGA Ch. Wks
D1 General Security Concepts Ch. 1, 6, 7, 10 Ch. 1, 2 Wks 6–7
D2 Threats, Vulns & Mitigations Ch. 2, 3, 4, 5 Ch. 3, 4, 5 Wks 8–10
D3 Security Architecture Ch. 11, 12, 13, 14 Ch. 6, 7 Wks 10–11
D4 Security Operations Ch. 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, 20 Ch. 8, 9, 10, 11 Wks 12–14
D5 Program Management Ch. 21, 22, 23, 24 Ch. 12, 13 Wks 14–15

Quick Reference
📌
Common ports, protocols, acronyms and PBQ patterns you need cold on exam day. Bookmark this tab for the last two weeks of study.
Common Ports to Know
20/21 — FTP (data/control)
22 — SSH / SFTP / SCP
23 — Telnet (insecure)
25 — SMTP
53 — DNS
67/68 — DHCP
80 — HTTP
88 — Kerberos
110 — POP3
119 — NNTP
123 — NTP
135 — RPC / WMI
137–139 — NetBIOS
143 — IMAP
161/162 — SNMP
389 — LDAP
443 — HTTPS / TLS
445 — SMB / CIFS
465/587 — SMTPS
500 — IKE / IPSec
514 — Syslog
636 — LDAPS
993 — IMAPS
995 — POP3S
1433 — MSSQL
1521 — Oracle DB
1723 — PPTP
3306 — MySQL
3389 — RDP
5060/5061 — SIP / SIPS
6514 — Syslog/TLS
8080/8443 — HTTP alt / HTTPS alt
Encryption Algorithms Cheat Sheet
AES — Symmetric block · 128/192/256-bit · FIPS approved
RSA — Asymmetric · 2048/4096-bit · key exchange, signatures
ECC — Asymmetric · smaller keys, mobile/IoT use
DES/3DES — Legacy symmetric · deprecated
SHA-256/512 — Hashing · FIPS approved
MD5 — Hashing · legacy, NOT for security
Diffie-Hellman — Key exchange (not encryption)
ECDHE — Ephemeral DH over ECC · perfect forward secrecy
PBKDF2/bcrypt — Password hashing / key stretching
Common PBQ Scenarios
Firewall rules — Read ACL rules top-down; first match wins. Know implicit deny-all at end.
Network diagrams — Identify DMZ placement, screened subnet, trust zones. WAF in front of web servers.
Log analysis — Impossible travel = same account, two geos, short time. Account lockout = spraying/brute force. Out-of-hours = insider threat.
Incident response order — Preparation → Detection → Analysis → Containment → Eradication → Recovery → Lessons Learned.
Risk formulas — SLE = Asset Value × Exposure Factor. ALE = SLE × ARO. RTO = how fast back online. RPO = how much data loss acceptable.
Certificate chain — Root CA → Intermediate CA → End-entity cert. OCSP stapling eliminates real-time CA lookup.
📋 Study Notes & Comparisons
📋
Detailed study notes and concept comparisons from your vocab list — formatted for easy reading.
D1 — General Security Concepts
IDEA [International Data Encryption Algorithm]
Old symmetric encryption algorithm used in early PGP A symmetric encryption algorithm that encrypts data in fixed-size blocks using the same key for both encryption and decryption
What is a downgrade attack?
An attack that tricks a device into using an older, weaker security protocol, like SSL instead of TLS, so the attacker can more easily intercept, read, or change the data traveling over the network.
What is a birthday attack?
It’s when an attacker finds two different inputs with the same hash value and uses that to trick systems that rely on hashing.
What is the difference between an IPS and a firewall?
A firewall filters incoming/outgoing traffic based on ports, IPs, protocols, and rules An IPS detects and blocks malicious activity based on traffic patterns and attack signatures
What is Assignment, Accounting, Ownership, and Classification?
Assignment: Giving someone the right to use something (like giving them keys to a room) Accounting: Keeping track of who accessed what and when Ownership: Saying who is responsible for a resource Classification: Labeling data based on how sensitive it is
Environmental Variables (in vulnerability management)
env vars can leak secrets if not locked down System configuration values that, if misconfigured or exposed, can leak sensitive data or lead to exploitable conditions Environmental variables are little data settings stored by your operating system — they tell programs where to find stuff, how to run, or what credentials to use. The problem is: if these variables are misconfigured or exposed , they can create security holes .
What are some key activities of Validation of remediation?
Rescanning
Run vulnerability scans again to verify the previous weaknesses no longer appear.
Audit
Perform inspections, or assessments to check if remediation policies and actions were properly followed.
Verification
Confirm through testing or manual checks that the fix works as intended.
What is the difference between a digital certificate and a digital signature?
Digital Certificate: Proves who you are and provides a public key Digital Signature: Proves the files was really sent by you and wasn’t changed. Created using sender’s private ke y and verified using sender’s public key.
What is the difference between hard and soft authentication tokens?
Hard token
A physical device that generates or displays a one-time authentication code (e.g.,
RSA SecurID key fob) Pros
Physical, harder to hack remotely
Cons
Can be lost, damaged, or costly to distribute
Soft token
A software application (often on a smartphone) that generates time-based or event-based one-time passwords (e.g.,
Google Authenticator app) Pros
Convenient, no extra hardware needed
Cons
Vulnerable if phone is compromised or malware present
💡 Please Dont Allow Cyber Evil Return Later
What is the process of incident response in order?
Preparation
Getting ready in advance - training your team, setting policies, configuring tools, and making backups
Detection
Spotting signs of an incident - alerts, logs, user reports,
IDS notifications Analysis
Investigating to confirm the incident and determind scope, impact, and method of attack.
Containment
Stopping the threat from spreading - isolate systems, disable accounts, or segment the network.
Eradication
Fully removing the threat - deleting malware, closing vulnerabilities, or resetting systems
Recovery
Bringing systems back online safely - restore backups, apply patches, verify stability.
Lessons Learned
Reviewing the incident afterward - what worked, what didn’t, and how to improve the response next time.
What is the difference between SOW and WO?
SOW (Statement of Work)
Almost like a detailed blueprint for a specific job. It explains exactly what’s being done, who’s doing it, when it’s due, and how it will be delivered.
Penetration test project plan WO (Work Order)
A document that authorizes a specific task or service to be performed, usually under an existing agreement or contract
More common for routine or maintenance work Example
“Set up 5 laptops” request
D2 — Threats, Vulnerabilities & Mitigations
What is the difference between client-based and agentless?
Client-Based (agent-based) Requires software (agent) installed on the device Provides deep, real-time monitoring and control. Used for tools like antivirus or endpoint detection Agentless: No software installed on the device Uses remote protocols (SSH, SNMP, API) for access Used for tasks like vulnerability scanning or basic monitoring (Nmap)
What is the difference between an Amplified and a Reflected DDoS attack?
Reflected
A type of attack where the attacker spoofs the victim’s IP and sends requests to third-party servers, which unknowingly send their responses to the victim, overwhelming them.
Amplified
A DDoS attack where small requests sent by the attacker generate disproportionately large responses from third-party servers, greatly increasing the attack’s volume Amplified builds off of Reflected . In both cases, the victim\'s IP is being spoofed
What is the difference between a firewall and an IPS?
Firewall
Checks where traffic is coming from, where it’s going, and what type of traffic it is — basically looking at addresses, ports, and protocols . It’s like checking IDs and deciding if someone’s allowed through the gate.
IPS
Looks at the behavior inside the traffic — it inspects the content or actions happening in the data to see if someone is trying to do something harmful (like exploit a vulnerability or inject malware).
What is the difference between Inline devices and Tap/Monitor devices?
Inline devices sit directly in the path of network traffic and can actively block or modify it IPS or firewalls - can prevent threats by actively controlling traffic Tap/Monitor devices passively copy traffic for analysis without interfering with the actual data flow IDS or packet sniffers - only observe traffic and alert but do not block
What is the difference between penetration testing and red teaming?
Pentesting
A simulated cyberattack against a specific system or application to identify and exploit vulnerabilities.
Last days to weeks Red Teaming
A full-scope, realistic simulation that tests an organization’s overall security posture, including defences, detection, and response. Lasts weeks to months
What is the difference Qualitative and Quantitative risk analysis?
Qualitative risk analysis
A method of assessing risk using descriptive ratings like high, medium, or low. “A cyberattack is likely and would have a high impact”
Quantitative risk analysis
A method of assessing risk using numbers, such as dollar loss or probablility percentages. “A data breach could cost us $250,000 and has a 20% chance of happening this year”
What is the difference between offensive and defensive penetration testing?
Offensive pentesting: You think like an attacker. Your goal is to break in and exploit weaknesses to find where security fails. Often performed by external consultants or red teams hired for the job Defensive pentesting: You think like a defender. Your goal is to find weaknesses yourself first and fix them before a bad actor can exploit them. Often done internally by the organization’s security or blue team.
D3 — Security Architecture
What is the difference between active devices and passive devices?
Active devices send, receive, and sometimes modify or generate network traffic. Firewalls, routers, and IPS - They can block or change traffic Passive devices only monitor or listen to network traffic without interfering or altering it. Network taps, IDS, and packet sniffers - they monitor traffic without affecting it
What is layer 4 of the OSI model?
Layer 4 (Transport Layer) : Handles the delivery of data between devices using TCP/UDP ports. It does not understand the content, just the port, IP, and delivery. Layer 4 firewalls filter based on IP addresses and port numbers Layer 4 just sees where traffic is going, for example, “port 80
What is the difference between load balancing and clustering?
Load Balancing: A method of distributing traffic or workload across multiple servers to improve performance and availability. Used to optimize performance Prevents overload on one server Clustering: A group of servers working together as a single system to provide high availability and failover in case one node fails Focuses on availability and fault tolerance (keeps working even when things break) If one server fails, another takes over immediately Good for critical services like databases
What is the difference between stateless and stateful firewalls?
Stateless
Filters each packet independently without remembering anything about previous packets; faster but less secure.
Stateful
Tracks active connections and filters packets based on connection context; more secure and accurate.
What is the difference between firewall rules and ACLs?
Firewall Rules
Broader and more complex rules used by firewalls to allow, deny, or log traffic. Often stateful and can inspect many details like IP, port, protocol, application data, and connection state.
Access Control Lists (ACLs)
Simpler, stateless filtering rules used mainly by routers and switches to allow or deny traffic based on IP addresses, ports, and protocols only.
What is the difference between Expansionary, Conservative, and Neutral Risk appetite?
Expansionary
“Let’s take big risks and grow fast”
Conservative
“Play it safe and avoid risk”
Neutral
“Balance between risk and caution”
D4 — Security Operations
What is the difference between a Hot, Warm, and Cold site?
Hot Site
A fully equipped backup site that is ready to take over operations immediately with minimal or no downtime.
Warm Site
A partially configured site with some equipment and data; requires some setup before use.
Cold Site
An empty facility with no active hardware or data; requires full setup and restoration before operations can resume.
What is the difference between “inventory” and “asset tracking”
Inventory
A static record of all IT assets, including hardware and software.
Asset Tracking
A dynamic process of monitoring the location, usage, ownership, and lifecycle status of IT assets
What are Data Custodians and Data Stewards?
Data Custodian
They tech person/team responsible for the day to day hands-on work like backups, updates and permissions enforcement.
Data Steward
More focused on ensuring data quality, accuracy and proper use They basically make sure names aren’t misspelled, phone numbers are in the right format, and the data follows standards..
What is data inventory and retention?
Data Inventory helps you know what data you have\ Data Retention help you decide how long to keep it before deleting it.
D5 — Program Management
What is the difference between a supplier and a vendor?
Supplier
A business that provides raw materials, components, or goods used in your products or services.
Gives you stuff you use to build your product Vendor
A business that provides services, tools, or finished products to help run your business (often IT-related) Gives you tools/services to help run your business
What is data sovereignty?
Data sovereignty means that data is subject to the laws and regulations of the country where it is physically stored, even if it belongs to a person or business in another country.
What are permission assignments and implications?
Permission assignments: The process of granting users access to resources based on their roles or needs Implications: The potential security risks or consequences that arise from misconfigured or excessive permissions.
What is the difference between Exception and Exemption?
Exception: Temporary or conditional permission to bypass a security control or policy with risk acceptance and compensating measures Exemption: Formal, often long-term permission to not comply with a specific security control or policy Both involve documented approvals and risk acceptance from management
What is the difference between internal and external Compliance reporting?
Internal compliance reporting
Tracks and communicates compliance status within the organization for internal management, audit, and policy enforcement. Reports for internal use to track policy & compliance
External compliance reporting
Provides documented proof to outside parties (like regulators or customers) that the organization meets legal, contractual, or industry-specific compliance requirements. Reports for outsiders to prove legal/contractual compliance
What is Attestation and acknowledgment?
When a user confirms in writing that they’ve read, understood, or agree to a policy or requirement Used in: security awareness, training, compliance, internal audits.
General
What is critical data?
Critical data is data that is essential to operations or safety, if it’s lost, corrupted, or unavailable, it could cause serious harm to the business or system It’s basically data that a company can’t fully function without
Geographic restrictions, geo-blocking, geo-fencing
Location-based access control Security controls that limit access to systems, services, or data based on the geographic location of a user or device.
What is Sanitization, Destruction, Certification, and Data Retention?
Sanitization: The process of securely removing all data from a device so it cannot be recovered Destruction: The physical or digital destruction of media so data is completely irrecoverable Certification: Documented confirmation that sanitization or destruction procedures were successfully performed Data Retention: Policies that dictate how long data must be stored before it can be deleted or archived
What does Provisioning and Deprovisioning user accounts mean?
Provisioning: The process of creating user accounts and assigning appropriate access and permissions De-provisioning: The process of removing user accounts and revoking access when no longer needed.
Full Resource Stack
💰
Budget summary: ~$180 in study materials + $425 exam fee = ~$605 total (or ~$540–560 via authorized partner voucher). With $200–$300/month, you can front-load the materials in month 1 and bank the rest toward the exam. Tip: buy your voucher through a CompTIA authorized partner for ~$360–380 instead of the full $425 retail price.
Resource Cost Phase Notes
Google Cybersecurity Certificate
Blacks in Technology · Open on Coursera →		 Free P1 Free through Blacks in Technology. Courses 1, 2, 3, 5, 6 only.
Professor Messer SY0-701 Video Course
professormesser.com · YouTube Playlist →		 Free P2 121 videos, ~15 hours. Core study resource. Watch actively — take notes.
Anki SY0-701 — Personal Security+ Vocab
307 terms · Download .apkg →		 Free P2 307 terms. Run 10 min/day throughout Phase 2.
Anki SY0-701 Acronym Deck
321 acronyms · Download .apkg →		 Free P2 321 acronyms. Add to daily Anki rotation once started.
CompTIA Security+ Study Guide, 9th Ed. (Sybex)
Mike Chapple & David Seidl · Open in Drive →		 Free P2 Deep conceptual reference — read chapter-per-domain alongside Messer. Includes practice exam. Use for understanding; pair with GCGA for exam-style questions on the same material.
Get Certified Get Ahead (GCGA) SY0-701
Gibson & Shelley · Open in Drive →		 Free P2 Mirrors CompTIA's question style and phrasing closely. Use end-of-chapter practice Qs after each Messer domain. Strong on PBQs. Use alongside Sybex — you have both free.
Professor Messer SY0-701 Course Notes
96 pages · Open in Drive →		 Free P2 PDF mirroring every video. Good for reviewing without rewatching. Optional but efficient.
CompTIA CertMaster Learn SY0-701
comptia.org		 $149/mo or $299/yr P2 CompTIA's official adaptive platform. Performance analytics show exact weak domains. Activate for 1 month during heaviest study — cancel after. Labs included.
Jason Dion Practice Exams — Set 1
6 exams · 540 Qs · Udemy →		 $13.99 P4 6 full 90-min timed exams, 90 Qs each including PBQs. Harder than the real exam — intentional. Per-question feedback with domain mapping.
Jason Dion Practice Exams — Set 2
6 exams · 540 Qs · Udemy →		 $16.99 P4 Completely different question set from Set 1. Same format — 6 exams × 90 Qs, timed, with PBQs and per-question domain feedback.
Professor Messer Practice Exams (A, B, C)
v1.6 · 3 × 90 Qs · Open in Drive →		 Free P4 Closest to real exam style and phrasing. Different question set from Dion — together they cover a wide range of material angles.
CompTIA Security+ SY0-701 Exam
pearsonvue.com/comptia		 $425 retail
~$360–380 via partner		 EXAM Schedule only after hitting 85%+ across 3+ full practice sets on different days. 750/900 to pass.

Practice Exam Gauntlet

⚠️
Rule: After each practice exam, don't just check your score — review every wrong answer and understand why it was wrong. One focused review session is worth more than three untreated exams.
